title: Does Gendo represent Japan on the Seele Council
display_title: Does Gendo represent Japan on the Seele Council
wikitext:
[]: Does Gendo "represent" Japan on the Seele Council?             Quote Post by V » Sat Jun 21, 2008 9:39 pm The Human Instrumentality Committee of the Seele Council consists of Germany (Keel), Russia, France, Britain, United States. Does Gendo "represent" Japan or something? Obviously, Gendo is not a member of the 12-man "Seele Council", but as for the "Human Instrumentality Committee" they do speak of it as if he has a "seat"....in that they threaten to take AWAY his place at the Committee if he misbehaves, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 21, 2008 9:50 pm Gendo isn't part of the committee.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:33 am So he's just the committee's Instrumentality bitch then?

[]:             Quote Post by pmkava » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:10 pm didn't at some point in the show they said that Yui's dad was on the Seele committee. in which case, Yui's dad represents Japan on the Committee, or at some point he did.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:57 pm This is assuming that Yui's father was Japanese, or that he stayed in Japan. Given how SEELE seems to work, Yui's father could've adopted his wife's surname, or Ikari could be a pseudonym or something.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jun 22, 2008 1:36 pm pmkava wrote: didn't at some point in the show they said that Yui's dad was on the Seele committee. in which case, Yui's dad represents Japan on the Committee, or at some point he did.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 22, 2008 1:52 pm My crappy translation here for all to get exact quotes from. Yui is the daughter of an influential member of Seele, and, accordingly, was in the position of being able to understand their plans. Gendo, knowing this, became acquainted with her.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jun 22, 2008 7:35 pm Yes I have read this before, thank you for reposting it here for those that have not; what is your position on the question?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jun 22, 2008 7:40 pm Who's to say that Yui's dad didn't die, whether by a natural death, accident, or otherwise? Even if he's alive, he might have retired from Seele, or been replaced by a successor. Even if he's still with Seele right now, his influence on the present is near-nil.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jun 22, 2008 7:54 pm indeed, 15 years is a long time

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 22, 2008 7:59 pm ＠V: I reposted it because the thread seemed to need a refresher... I'm not sure what the "question" was that I didn't already post a response to. Ursus: My own assumption is that the guy is dead. Incidentally, if the senior Ikari had been "influential", in a good way, this might have predisposed Kiel towards favoring Gendo. If that makes any sense.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jun 22, 2008 8:10 pm That he's dead is the most likely, because to be "influential" and then not be mentioned in the slightest in the TV series doesn't make any sense at all. The point you raised about him influencing Keel in keeping Gendo is most interesting. If he'd been a close friend/partner of Keel, his influence would have been especially strong.
